lab.shareHint: Open this Nema Lab experiment from the same simulator state.

error.invalidBase64: Deep link: invalid base64 in config parameter.
error.invalidJSON: Deep link: config parameter is not valid JSON.
error.invalidConfigShape: Deep link: config parameter has unexpected shape.
error.unknownConfigError: Deep link: could not parse config parameter.

food-seeking.description: A baseline preset with chemotaxis enabled. It shows how local sensory sampling alone can bias the worm toward food.

touch-only.description: Touch sensing only. The worm can avoid obstacles, but it struggles to find food consistently.

chemo-bias.description: A stronger chemotaxis condition with higher gradient gain, pulling the worm toward the food patch more quickly.

thermal-belt.description: Combines a temperature gradient with a preferred temperature, producing behavior that tends to stay near a target band.

blind-rover.description: All sensors are disabled and the worm moves by exploration alone, making it easy to compare how inefficient unguided motion can be.

obstacle-field.description: A denser obstacle field designed to make touch-driven avoidance and turning events easier to observe.

saved.title: Saved Experiments
saved.emptyHint: Saved runs from Simulator will appear here for quick reload and cleanup.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Adjust interface behavior and manage saved experiment runs from one place.
settings.preferencesDescription: Tune language, contrast, motion, and onboarding behavior for the way you work.
settings.archiveDescription: Review, reload, or clear experiment snapshots stored locally on this device.

credits.title: Credit
credits.description: Project background, authorship, and a practical guide to reading the simulator, atlas, and comparison views.
credits.projectTitle: A research-framed sandbox for sensorimotor behavior
credits.projectBody1: Nema Lab is an interactive demo that reframes chemical cue response, thermal response, touch avoidance, and locomotion in C. elegans as a readable sensorimotor system, with a linked neural atlas visualized in real time.
credits.projectBody2: Within this app, the English labels "C. elegans" and "nematodes" are both used in the context of the same worm, 예쁜꼬마선충. Rather than aiming for a publication-grade biological reconstruction, it focuses on making the relationship between sensory cues, internal state, and observable movement legible across Simulator, Connectome, Synchrony, and Compare.

simulator.title: Simulator
simulator.body: The main workspace for adjusting experiment settings, running the worm, and monitoring Tracking, State, and Sensor Summary in real time.

connectome.title: Connectome
connectome.body: An interpretation view that combines Live Inputs, Behavior Snapshot, Dominant Route, Neural Atlas, 2D View, and Selected Neuron.

synchrony.title: Synchrony
synchrony.body: A synchronized monitoring view that aligns Tracking Camera, Neural Atlas, and Environment Arena to the same moment.

compare.title: Compare
compare.body: A comparison view that places Baseline (A) and Variant (B) side by side under matched playback conditions.

library.title: Library
library.body: A preset browser for quickly launching representative behavior presets and using them as starting points for new experiments.

credits.notesDescription: A few practical notes help set expectations for how the demo should be read.

items.research: This app is research-based and educational in tone. It is intended to support exploration and communication, not to replace experimental measurement or publication-grade modeling.
items.storage: Saved runs are stored locally in the current browser environment, so they may not automatically transfer across devices or profiles.
items.contact: Questions, feedback, or collaboration inquiries can be sent to the email listed above.

landing.title: Nema Lab
landing.subtitle: C. elegans Behavior & Neural Simulator
landing.description: Nema Lab is an interactive demo that reframes chemical cue response, thermal response, touch avoidance, and locomotion in C. elegans as a readable sensorimotor system, with a linked neural atlas visualized in real time.

toast.linkCopyFailed: Failed to copy the share link.
toast.copiedToCompare: Simulator config copied to Compare baseline.

tooltip.touch: Detects physical contact with obstacles/walls to trigger avoidance behavior
tooltip.chemo: Senses chemical concentration gradient from food to bias movement toward it
tooltip.thermo: Senses temperature gradient to navigate toward preferred temperature band
tooltip.showTrail: Displays the worm's movement path as a trail
tooltip.showChemicalOverlay: Visualizes the food chemical concentration field
tooltip.showTemperatureOverlay: Visualizes the temperature gradient as color
tooltip.showSensors: Shows left/right sensor sample point positions
tooltip.showEventMarkers: Marks collision, turn, and other event locations
tooltip.cleanMode: Hides grid, borders, and other auxiliary elements for a clean view

tracking3d.controlsHint: Drag to orbit, wheel to zoom, right-drag to pan
tracking3d.sceneNote: NGM agar surface, OP50 lawn, shallow observation chamber
